title: Wind-Blown Wonders
synopsis: A shy little ghost discovers an unexpected gift at her lighthouse home, leading to three days of hilarious mishaps as she learns to name her swirling emotions.

chapters.title: The Package That Wouldn't Stay Put

pages.text: The wind howled like fifty hungry wolves. It rattled every window in the lighthouse. It knocked over flower pots. It stole hats from seagulls. The lighthouse swayed back and forth. Back and forth. Like a giant rocking chair. A cheerful little ghost named Boo peeked out the round window at the very top. Her wispy body trembled. Not from the cold. From something fluttery in her middle.

illustration.description: A cheerful little ghost with a soft glowing-white wispy body, round rosy-pink cheeks, and big kind blue eyes floating at a round lighthouse window. Outside, fierce winds bend trees sideways and send flower pots tumbling. Seagulls chase their blown-away hats. The lighthouse tilts dramatically. Spring pastel colors of soft yellow, pink, and green show the windy chaos.
illustration.alt: Boo the ghost looking out a lighthouse window during a fierce windstorm

pages.text: THUNK. Something hit the lighthouse door. THUNK THUNK. Boo floated down the spiral stairs. Around and around and around. She got dizzy. She bumped into the railing. She spun like a top. Finally she reached the bottom. A bright purple package sat by the door. The wind tried to steal it. It rolled left. It rolled right. It did three somersaults.

illustration.description: A cheerful little ghost with a soft glowing-white wispy body, round rosy-pink cheeks, and big kind blue eyes spinning dizzily on spiral lighthouse stairs. At the bottom, a bright purple package rolls and tumbles by the door as wind whips through cracks. Summer bright colors of purple, orange, and turquoise show the chaotic movement and Boo's dizzy descent.
illustration.alt: Boo spinning down lighthouse stairs while a purple package tumbles below

pages.text: Day One was discovering. Boo caught the runaway package. She read the label. 'For the Lighthouse Friend.' No name on it. No return address. Just those three words. Boo felt that fluttery feeling again. Stronger now. Like butterflies doing backflips. She had never gotten a gift from someone she didn't know. The package wiggled. Something was alive inside.

illustration.description: A cheerful little ghost with a soft glowing-white wispy body, round rosy-pink cheeks, and big kind blue eyes holding a wiggling purple package close to her wispy form. The package has a simple label reading 'For the Lighthouse Friend'. Autumn warm colors of orange, gold, and deep red create a cozy glow around the lighthouse interior as the mysterious package seems to move on its own.
illustration.alt: Boo holding a wiggling mysterious package addressed to 'Lighthouse Friend'

pages.text: Day Two brought problems. Big ones. Boo tried to open the package. The ribbon was tied in seventeen knots. She pulled with her ghostly hands. The ribbon snapped back. BOING. It hit her in the nose. She tumbled backward. She crashed into a bucket. The bucket flew across the room. It bonked against the lighthouse lamp. The lamp started spinning. Round and round.

illustration.description: A cheerful little ghost with a soft glowing-white wispy body, round rosy-pink cheeks, and big kind blue eyes tumbling backward after a ribbon snapped and hit her nose. A bucket flies through the air toward a spinning lighthouse lamp. Winter cool colors of icy blue, silver, and pale purple show the slapstick chaos as objects bounce and spin around the lighthouse interior.
illustration.alt: Boo tumbling backward as a ribbon snaps and objects crash around the lighthouse

pages.text: The spinning light made crazy shadows. They danced on the walls. They looked like monsters. Boo felt scared. Her heart thumped loud. THUMP THUMP THUMP. She tried to stop the lamp. She pushed it. It spun faster. She pulled it. It spun even faster. The shadows grew bigger. They wiggled their shadow fingers. The butterflies in Boo's middle turned into jumping frogs.

illustration.description: A cheerful little ghost with a soft glowing-white wispy body, round rosy-pink cheeks, and big kind blue eyes reaching toward a wildly spinning lighthouse lamp. Giant dancing shadows that look like monsters cover the walls, wiggling their shadow fingers. The same scene shows the passage of seasons with spring pastels blending into summer brights, showing how Boo's emotions change like the seasons.
illustration.alt: Boo trying to stop a spinning lamp while monster-like shadows dance on the walls

pages.text: Day Three meant choosing. The package still wiggled. The shadows still danced. The lamp still spun. Boo sat in the corner. She felt all mixed up inside. Happy and scared and curious and worried. All at the same time. Like a soup made of feelings. She took a deep breath. She floated to the package. This time she didn't fight the knots. She asked nicely. 'Please untie yourselves.'

illustration.description: A cheerful little ghost with a soft glowing-white wispy body, round rosy-pink cheeks, and big kind blue eyes sitting peacefully in a lighthouse corner, gently speaking to the knotted purple package. The scene transitions through all seasons - autumn warmth showing her contemplation, winter cool showing her calm decision, spring pastels showing new hope, and summer brights showing her courage to try a gentle approach.
illustration.alt: Boo sitting calmly and gently asking the knotted package to untie itself

pages.text: The knots listened. They wiggled loose. The package opened. Inside was a tiny wind chime. Made of seashells and sea glass. It sang the most beautiful song. The lamp slowed down. The shadows became gentle. The butterflies in Boo's middle settled into a warm, happy flutter. She hung the chime by her window. Now she had a friend to sing with the wind. A gift from someone who knew exactly what she needed.

illustration.description: A cheerful little ghost with a soft glowing-white wispy body, round rosy-pink cheeks, and big kind blue eyes hanging a delicate wind chime made of seashells and sea glass by the lighthouse window. The lamp glows steadily, casting gentle shadows. The cyclical beauty of all seasons surrounds the lighthouse - spring flowers, summer sunshine, autumn leaves, and winter snow - showing how the wind chime will sing through every season as nature's calendar turns.
illustration.alt: Boo hanging a beautiful seashell wind chime by her lighthouse window

coverPrompt.description: A cheerful little ghost with a soft glowing-white wispy body, round rosy-pink cheeks, and big kind blue eyes floating near a tall lighthouse perched on rocky cliffs. Wild winds blow colorful objects through the air - flower petals, leaves, ribbons, and small parcels. The lighthouse beam cuts through swirling clouds in spring pastel colors of soft pink, mint green, and lavender.
coverPrompt.alt: A friendly ghost at a windy lighthouse with colorful objects blowing in the wind

questions.question: What did Boo feel in her middle when she first saw the package?
questions.explanation: Boo felt butterflies doing backflips in her middle - that fluttery, excited feeling when something surprising happens.

questions.question: How did Boo finally get the package open?
questions.explanation: Instead of fighting the knots, Boo tried being gentle and asked them nicely to untie themselves, and it worked!

questions.question: What was inside the mysterious package?
questions.explanation: The package contained a beautiful wind chime made of seashells and sea glass that sang with the wind.

questions.question: How did Boo feel at the end - happy, scared, or mixed up?
questions.explanation: By the end, Boo felt happy and peaceful. The butterflies in her middle became a warm, happy flutter when she heard the wind chime's beautiful song.
